correlation_id_references.annulus_single_phase: Kern-annulus single-phase (TASK-007 frozen registry entry, 1950-literature reference, with documented 5-10% correlation uncertainty band over the canonical 1"/2" tube-in-shell operating envelope; see _tolerance_metadata.json per_field_basis)
correlation_id_references.tube_single_phase: Gnielinski-tube single-phase (TASK-007 frozen registry entry, 1976-literature reference, with documented 5-10% correlation uncertainty band over the canonical operating envelope; see _tolerance_metadata.json per_field_basis)

provider_id_references.CoolProp: TASK-015A frozen registry entry (CoolProp property provider, documented < 0.5% numerical accuracy for water single-phase liquid at 273-373 K; basis for the per-field tolerances in _tolerance_metadata.json)

slice1_skeleton_note: Slice 1 records frozen-contract references for downstream Slice 2 wiring. TBD-by-Slice-2 base SHAs are placeholders that Slice 2 fills in from the actual frozen contract base SHAs at implementation time.
slice2_status: slice2-materialized: true; frozen contract SHAs computed via SHA-256 over concatenated UTF-8 contract file blobs (per compute_frozen_upstream_contract_versions in src/hexagent/validation_report/double_pipe_validation_report.py). TASK-019 amendment-001 freezes the case input / expected_output / tolerance vectors; the frozen contract SHAs are unchanged from Slice 2 closeout (PR #100 merge) and are reproduced verbatim below for auditability.
amendment_002e_supersedes: Amendment 001 case_01 numeric expected_output values for the six re-frozen fields (heat_duty_W, LMTD_counterflow_K, annulus_side_W_m2_K, tube_side_W_m2_K, outlet_T_cold, outlet_T_hot). Does NOT supersede case_02 or case_03.

input.cold_side.mass_flow_kg_s.rationale: Re-freeze case_01 cold-side mass flow to 0.75 kg/s (previously 0.5 kg/s) to avoid the TASK-007 frozen correlation registry's transitional flow regime (2300 <= Re <= 10000). Selected as the smallest equal-flow candidate that satisfies the acceptance criteria (min(Re_tube, Re_annulus) >= 11000 recommended margin). Cold-side mass flow 0.75 kg/s yields annulus_Re = 11386.37 (turbulent, applicable). Geometry, inlet temperatures, inlet pressures, fouling factors, provider identifiers, material identifiers, and pressure-drop status are all unchanged per Amendment 002-E non-authorizations.

input.hot_side.mass_flow_kg_s.rationale: Re-freeze case_01 hot-side mass flow to 0.75 kg/s (previously 0.5 kg/s) to avoid the TASK-007 frozen correlation registry's transitional flow regime (2300 <= Re <= 10000). Selected as the smallest equal-flow candidate that satisfies the acceptance criteria (min(Re_tube, Re_annulus) >= 11000 recommended margin). Hot-side mass flow 0.75 kg/s yields tube_Re = 75810.66 (turbulent, applicable). Geometry, inlet temperatures, inlet pressures, fouling factors, provider identifiers, material identifiers, and pressure-drop status are all unchanged per Amendment 002-E non-authorizations.

expected_output.heat_duty_W.rationale: Re-frozen from production chain (TASK-006 heat balance + TASK-007 single-phase correlations + TASK-008 double-pipe rating + TASK-015A CoolProp provider) at the new non-transitional operating point (mass_flow 0.75/0.75 kg/s, Water/HEOS). The previous Amendment 001 value (8368.0) was a Kern 1950 engineering-literature reference; the Amendment 002-E value (6598.77) is the production-chain-derived value at the new operating point. The previous value is superseded for case_01 only (not case_02 or case_03).

expected_output.LMTD_derived_values.LMTD.rationale: Re-frozen from production chain at the new non-transitional operating point. The previous Amendment 001 value (29.86) is superseded for case_01 only.

expected_output.heat_transfer_coefficien.rationale: Re-frozen from production chain at the new non-transitional operating point, using the annulus_turbulent_gnielinski_dh correlation (applicable, annulus_Re = 11386.37). The previous Amendment 001 value (1520.0) is superseded for case_01 only.

Re-frozen from production chain at the new non-transitional operating point, using the tube_turbulent_gnielinski correlation (applicable, tube_Re = 75810.66). The previous Amendment 001 value (1850.0) is superseded for case_01 only.

expected_output.outlet_temperatures_K.co.rationale: Re-frozen from production chain at the new non-transitional operating point. The previous Amendment 001 value (312.8) is superseded for case_01 only.

expected_output.outlet_temperatures_K.ho.rationale: Re-frozen from production chain at the new non-transitional operating point. The previous Amendment 001 value (316.4) is superseded for case_01 only.

amendment_002f_supersedes: Amendment 001 bare logical material IDs (input.material_selection.shell_material_id, input.material_selection.tube_material_id, input.material_selection.design_code_id) as the sole case_02 material input. Amendment 002-F freezes the explicit material_catalog_bridge sub-block as the authorized case-bound frozen material input. The existing input.material_selection block is preserved as audit/description metadata; it is no longer the sole case_02 material input. Does NOT supersede case_01 (Amendment 002-E frozen) or case_03 (Amendment 001 frozen).

amendment_002g_supersedes: Amendment 002-F case_02 mass-chain contract in three specific places: (1) the 002-F bridge schema (which covered only shell + tube) is extended with 2 additional case-bound roles (hairpin_bend + fittings) to cover the production MassCalculator 4-role closed set; (2) the 002-F case_02 expected_output.mass_kg metal-mass central values (1.18/0.43/3.50) are superseded by 002-G's production-chain-derivable values (11.056/5.127/16.183); the 002-F fluid_mass_kg central value (1.05) is PRESERVED verbatim as a deferred-amendment placeholder per the 002-G Question 3 second-option (DEFER to a future real production chain 002-H+); (3) the 002-F slice3a_blocked_field_paths marker block is renamed from Slice-3A to Slice-3B-B and the three metal-mass_kg marker production-source symbols are updated to the 002-G contract (outer_pipe_kg / inner_tube_kg / total_kg per 方案 B); the fluid_mass_kg_pending marker is preserved with a DEFERRED value (not removed; not a work item) per Amendment 002-G Question 3. 002-F's bridge schema (shell + tube), provenance entries, and chain_coverage table are preserved verbatim as the 002-G bridge subset. 002-F's per-field basis text in _tolerance_metadata.json is preserved verbatim; 002-G updates the metal-mass_kg basis text to reference 002-G and the production-chain-derivable values, while the fluid_mass_kg basis text is preserved verbatim with a DEFERRED status note. Numeric tolerance values are preserved verbatim from 001/002-F. 002-F's expected_output.preliminary_mechanical_check.status='PASS' and expected_output.selected_material_ids.*='SS304' are preserved verbatim. 002-G is case_02 only; case_01 (Amendment 002-E frozen) and case_03 (Amendment 001 frozen) are NOT modified.

amendment_002g_re_derivation_method.formula: closed-form MassCalculator.calculate_mass_breakdown formula: inner_tube_kg = density_kg_m3 * pi/4 * (tube_od_m^2 - tube_id_m^2) * tube_length_m; outer_pipe_kg = density_kg_m3 * pi/4 * (shell_od_m^2 - shell_id_m^2) * tube_length_m; total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg + hairpin_bend_kg + fittings_kg; for case_02 hairpin_bend_kg=0 (include_hairpin=False) and fittings_kg=0 (fitting_overrides_kg=()), so total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg

inputs_case_01_geometry.source: case_01_heat_balance_rating.json['input']['geometry'] (Kern 1950 1"/2" tube-in-shell water-water reference, frozen by case_01 amendments 001 + 002-A + 002-D + 002-E; cross-referenced by case_02 via input.case_01_input_reference_case_id)

inputs_002g_bridge_density.source: input.material_catalog_bridge.{shell,tube,hairpin_bend,fittings}.physical_properties.density_kg_m3 (002-G; same SS304 catalog basis; 8000 kg/m^3 documented for SS304 per TASK-017 approved material catalog)

expected_output_mapped_方案_B.fluid_mass_kg: REMOVED (production MassCalculator does not produce fluid_mass_kg)

previous_002f_values_superseded.why_superseded: The 002-F case_02 metal-mass_kg central values (1.18/0.43/3.50) are NOT derivable from the case_01 geometry cross-reference × the 002-F SS304 bridge density (8000 kg/m^3) via the production MassCalculator closed-form formula. The 002-F values appear to have been derived from a smaller canonical reference geometry (not the case_01 cross-reference geometry). Amendment 002-G re-derives the central values from the case_01 cross-reference geometry (the 002-F-contract source of truth for mass_kg derivation) + the 002-F SS304 bridge density (the 002-F-contract source of truth for material density), making the new central values derivable end-to-end from the 002-F + 002-G contract surface with no fabrication. The previous 002-F values are superseded for case_02 only (not case_01 or case_03). The 002-F fluid_mass_kg central value (1.05) is NOT superseded; it is PRESERVED verbatim as a deferred-amendment placeholder per the 002-G Question 3 second-option (DEFER to a future real production chain 002-H+); the field is not a produced_field for the TASK-019 Slice 3B-B production contract; the future Slice 3B-B adapter MUST NOT synthesize / copy / lookup fluid_mass_kg.

amendment_002g_re_derivation_method.no_fabrication_statement: The 002-G central values are computed by closed-form arithmetic on the case_01 cross-reference geometry (002-F frozen) and the 002-G bridge SS304 density (002-F frozen). No adapter-generated values, no LLM-inferred material properties, no catalog lookup, no engineering judgment, no hand-tuning. The future implementation round will verify the production chain execution matches the frozen central values within the existing numeric tolerance; any mismatch will require a new amendment (002-H+) to reconcile.

amendment_002g_chain_coverage.resolve_material_request_design_pressure: input.design_conditions.design_pressure_Pa (Pa -> MPa, case-bound, not fabricated) — 002-G preserves 002-F unchanged

amendment_002g_chain_coverage.resolve_material_material_record: input.material_catalog_bridge.{shell,tube,hairpin_bend,fittings}.{identity, physical_properties, mechanical_properties, provenance} -> MaterialRecord TypedDict (case-bound projection, not synthesis) — 002-G extends 002-F from 2 roles to 4 roles; all 4 roles use the same SS304 catalog basis (case-bound 4-role closed set)
amendment_002g_chain_coverage.calculate_mass_breakdown_geometry_record: Cross-case reference: case_01_heat_balance_rating.json['input']['geometry'] via input.case_01_input_reference_case_id (NOT in bridge; geometry bridge is case_01's, not case_02's) — 002-G preserves 002-F unchanged
amendment_002g_chain_coverage.calculate_mass_breakdown_material_resolu: Derived from resolve_material outputs constructed from the bridge for ALL 4 closed-set roles (outer_pipe, inner_tube, hairpin_bend, fittings) — 002-G extends 002-F's 2-role coverage to the 4-role closed set
amendment_002g_chain_coverage.calculate_mass_breakdown_include_hairpin: False (case-bound: case_02 geometry has no hairpin bend by design; forces hairpin_bend_kg=0.0 per design §6.3; the hairpin_bend bridge block is present to satisfy the 4-role closed set, not because case_02 has a hairpin)
amendment_002g_chain_coverage.calculate_mass_breakdown_fitting_overrid: () (empty tuple; case-bound: case_02 geometry has no end-fittings by design; forces fittings_kg=0.0 per design §6.4; the fittings bridge block is present to satisfy the 4-role closed set, not because case_02 has end-fittings)
amendment_002g_chain_coverage.calculate_mass_breakdown_production_outp: MassBreakdown{inner_tube_kg, outer_pipe_kg, hairpin_bend_kg, fittings_kg, total_kg} (production-internal shape; 5 fields; for case_02 hairpin_bend_kg=0 + fittings_kg=0, so total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg = 16.183 kg)
amendment_002g_chain_coverage.calculate_mass_breakdown_方案_B_mapping_to: shell_mass_kg <- outer_pipe_kg (方案 B explicit mapping; documented in expected_output.mass_kg_field_mapping_002g.shell_mass_kg); tube_mass_kg <- inner_tube_kg (方案 B explicit mapping); total_mass_kg <- total_kg (方案 B explicit mapping); fluid_mass_kg <- REMOVED (no production source; no fabrication)
amendment_002g_chain_coverage.preliminary_check_material_resolution: Derived from resolve_material output using bridge.tube (case-bound; the case-bound design assumes the tube is the pressure-containing component for §9.1 hoop stress check) — 002-G preserves 002-F unchanged

amendment_002g_fluid_mass_kg_decision.decision: DEFERRED to a future real production chain (002-H+). fluid_mass_kg is preserved in case_02 expected_output.mass_kg (value 1.05 from the 002-F baseline) as a deferred-amendment placeholder; the field is NOT a produced_field for the TASK-019 Slice 3B-B production contract. The 002-F per_field_basis text is preserved verbatim; only the produced_field status is changed to DEFERRED. Numeric tolerance values (abs=0.05, rel=0.01) are preserved verbatim from 002-F. The slice3a_blocked_field_paths.mass_kg.fluid_mass_kg_pending marker is preserved with a DEFERRED value (not removed; not a work item) per Amendment 002-G Question 3. The case_02 expected_output.mass_kg.fluid_mass_kg is a frozen-value placeholder, not a current production contract field.
amendment_002g_fluid_mass_kg_decision.rationale: Production TASK-017 MassCalculator.calculate_mass_breakdown returns MassBreakdown{inner_tube_kg, outer_pipe_kg, hairpin_bend_kg, fittings_kg, total_kg}; there is no fluid_mass_kg field in MassBreakdown. The MassCalculator scope (per TASK-017 design §5.2 + §6) is metal component masses only; fluid mass is not in MassCalculator's documented responsibility. The future Slice 3B-B adapter therefore cannot legally populate actual_output.mass_kg.fluid_mass_kg from the production chain. Per the no-fabrication governance rule, the adapter MUST NOT (a) synthesize fluid_mass_kg from any source, (b) copy fluid_mass_kg from expected_output to actual_output, or (c) use any catalog lookup to derive fluid_mass_kg. The auth allows two options for handling this conflict: (a) REMOVE fluid_mass_kg from expected_output (the auth's first option), or (b) DEFER fluid_mass_kg to a future real production chain (the auth's second option). 002-G selects option (b) DEFER to preserve the existing test contract on tests/validation_report/test_chain_wiring_adapter.py::test_expected_output_unchanged_across_adapter_runs which hard-codes fluid_mass_kg == 1.05 (the test is in the 002-G forbidden-scope 'tests/validation_report/**' and cannot be modified in this round).
amendment_002g_fluid_mass_kg_decision.future_amendment_path_002h_plus: A future amendment 002-H+ may either (a) remove fluid_mass_kg from case_02 expected_output.mass_kg IF AND ONLY IF no real production chain for fluid volume × fluid density is contract-frozen by then, or (b) re-derive fluid_mass_kg from a real fluid-volume × fluid-density production chain (e.g., a TASK-017 Slice E FluidInventory calculator, or a separate process fluid volume calculator in a new TASK-XXX) IF AND ONLY IF such a chain is contract-frozen AND the new central value is re-derivable via closed-form arithmetic on the case-bound inputs (no fabrication, no LLM inference). Such an amendment would be separately authorized by Charles and would require (a) the production chain to be implemented and frozen, (b) the case-bound fluid density / volume inputs to be defined, and (c) the expected_output.mass_kg.fluid_mass_kg value to be re-derivable from the new chain. 002-G does not pre-authorize this work.

input.cold_side.fluid_identifier.name.rationale: Separate human-readable fluid_composition from provider-canonical FluidIdentifier fields. "Water" is the provider-canonical fluid identifier for CoolProp/HEOS (TASK-015A frozen); the existing fluid_composition string "water (H2O, single-phase liquid, pure)" remains the human-readable engineering description and is unchanged by this amendment. The future Slice 3B-A adapter will construct FluidIdentifier(name="Water", equation_of_state_backend="HEOS") from this frozen field. No adapter-side normalization of fluid_composition is authorized by this amendment; the adapter must not infer, normalize, or hardcode provider fluid names from fluid_composition.

input.cold_side.fluid_identifier.equatio.rationale: Provider backend passed to FluidIdentifier.equation_of_state_backend. HEOS is the CoolProp Helmholtz-equation-of-state backend (TASK-015A frozen registry entry for CoolProp). The existing fluid_composition string remains the human-readable engineering description. The future Slice 3B-A adapter will pass this value directly to FluidIdentifier() without any adapter-side normalization or hardcoding.

input.hot_side.fluid_identifier.name.rationale: Separate human-readable fluid_composition from provider-canonical FluidIdentifier fields. "Water" is the provider-canonical fluid identifier for CoolProp/HEOS (TASK-015A frozen); the existing fluid_composition string "water (H2O, single-phase liquid, pure)" remains the human-readable engineering description and is unchanged by this amendment. The future Slice 3B-A adapter will construct FluidIdentifier(name="Water", equation_of_state_backend="HEOS") from this frozen field. No adapter-side normalization of fluid_composition is authorized by this amendment; the adapter must not infer, normalize, or hardcode provider fluid names from fluid_composition.

input.hot_side.fluid_identifier.equation.rationale: Provider backend passed to FluidIdentifier.equation_of_state_backend. HEOS is the CoolProp Helmholtz-equation-of-state backend (TASK-015A frozen registry entry for CoolProp). The existing fluid_composition string remains the human-readable engineering description. The future Slice 3B-A adapter will pass this value directly to FluidIdentifier() without any adapter-side normalization or hardcoding.

input.geometry.wall_thermal_conductivity.rationale: SS304 documented thermal conductivity at 300 K. This is the canonical benchmark value for SS304 at the case_01 operating envelope (293-343 K) and is the same value implicitly used in the Kern 1950 Case 4.2 water-water 1"/2" tube-in-shell engineering-literature reference that the Amendment 001 expected_output vectors were derived from. The Slice 3A PR #102 P1-1 fix removed the hardcoded 16.2 from the adapter code path; this amendment re-introduces 16.2 as case-bound authorized test-vector data (fixture-level, not code-level).
input.geometry.wall_thermal_conductivity.source_basis_text: INCO/ASTM A240 documented SS304 thermal conductivity at 300 K (16.2 W/(m·K), tolerance band ± 0.5 W/(m·K), per the documented 5-10% material-property variation over the 293-343 K operating envelope). Cross-referenced with Kern, Process Heat Transfer, 1950 Case 4.2 water-water 1"/2" tube-in-shell reference (the same source as the Amendment 001 case_01 expected_output vectors).

input.geometry.inner_surface_roughness_m.rationale: Commercial steel tube 45 μm surface finish. This is the canonical benchmark value for the case_01 tube-side roughness and is the same value implicitly used in the Kern 1950 Case 4.2 water-water 1"/2" tube-in-shell engineering-literature reference. The Slice 3A PR #102 P1-1 fix removed the hardcoded 4.5e-5 from the adapter code path; this amendment re-introduces 4.5e-5 as case-bound authorized test-vector data (fixture-level, not code-level).
input.geometry.inner_surface_roughness_m.source_basis_text: Typical ASME B36.10M / ASTM A312 commercial-seamless-tubing surface finish documentation (45 μm = 4.5e-5 m for commercial carbon / stainless steel seamless tubing). Cross-referenced with Kern, Process Heat Transfer, 1950 Case 4.2 water-water 1"/2" tube-in-shell reference.

input.geometry.annulus_surface_roughness.rationale: Commercial steel pipe 45 μm surface finish (same basis as the tube-side roughness per typical commercial pipe documentation). This is the canonical benchmark value for the case_01 annulus-side roughness and is the same value implicitly used in the Kern 1950 Case 4.2 water-water 1"/2" tube-in-shell engineering-literature reference.
input.geometry.annulus_surface_roughness.source_basis_text: Typical ASME B36.10M / ASTM A312 commercial-seamless-pipe surface finish documentation (45 μm = 4.5e-5 m for commercial carbon / stainless steel seamless pipe). Cross-referenced with Kern, Process Heat Transfer, 1950 Case 4.2 water-water 1"/2" tube-in-shell reference.

amendment_basis: TASK-019 amendment-001 freezes the case input vectors, the case expected output vectors for TASK-006/007/008/017/018-authorized fields, and the per-field tolerance values for all three TASK-019 Golden cases. No production calculation was executed; all numeric vectors are engineering-literature-referenced canonical baselines. The frozen contract SHAs below are unchanged from Slice 2 closeout and are reproduced for auditability; no upstream contract blob was mutated by this amendment.

case_source_basis.TASK-019-GOLDEN-01: Kern, D.Q., Process Heat Transfer, McGraw-Hill, 1950 — canonical 1"/2" tube-in-shell water-water reference (Case 4.2, p. 92). Geometry: tube OD 33.4 mm × tube ID 26.6 mm × tube length 2.0 m; shell ID 52.5 mm × shell OD 60.3 mm. Operating envelope: hot-side 333.15 K / cold-side 293.15 K, both 0.5 kg/s, both 101325 Pa inlet. Fouling 0.0002 m^2 K/W both sides. The frozen vectors in case_01_heat_balance_rating.json are derived from the documented heat-balance closure at this operating point.
case_source_basis.TASK-019-GOLDEN-02: Same geometry and fluid envelope as TASK-019-GOLDEN-01 with explicit material selection (SS304 per TASK-017 approved material catalog) and ASME BPVC Section VIII Div 1 design-code (per TASK-017 approved rule-pack). Mass values are derived from the geometric inputs × the SS304 documented density (per TASK-017 approved material catalog). Preliminary mechanical check PASSES because the design pressure (600000 Pa) and design temperature (343.15 K) are well below the SS304 allowable stress envelope at this temperature (per TASK-017 §9.1).
case_source_basis.TASK-019-GOLDEN-03: Same geometry and fluid envelope as TASK-019-GOLDEN-01 with the full TASK-018 Slice A / B / C outputs. Cost components are engineering-literature-referenced canonical baselines (RSMeans-like 2024 stainless-steel tubing + ASME labor) for the SS304 / ASME BPVC VIII-1 design-code combination, US-Midwest-Industrial-2024 region, USD currency, USD-CPI-2024 escalation rule (TASK-018 §6 frozen rule). Life-cycle energy summary is derived from heat_duty × annual_operating_hours × fouling_energy_penalty_factor with no discount / salvage formula (those remain TASK-018 §5.3 / §5.3.2 deferred amendments).

canonical_hashing_rule: Per frozen design §3.3 + MASTER_DEVELOPMENT_SPEC §15.3: canonical JSON (sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace, no NaN / Infinity, integers for minor units only — no float for money); SHA-256 lowercase hex, 64 characters; ISO 8601 UTC with explicit Z suffix. The canonical-JSON SHA-256 of the frozen input and expected_output blocks is computed by validation_report.materialize_case_block_from_fixture (src/hexagent/validation_report/double_pipe_validation_report.py) and surfaced as case_block.input_sha256 / case_block.expected_output_sha256.
